system.description: Gravity UI is a comprehensive open-source design system and component ecosystem built by Yandex for production-grade web applications. It provides a unified visual language across a large suite of React libraries covering UI primitives, navigation, data visualization, forms, editors, dashboards, AI chat interfaces, server-side tooling, and developer tooling configs. All components share a consistent theming model, spacing scale, and typography system based on CSS custom properties.
system.theming: Theming is controlled via the ThemeProvider component from @gravity-ui/uikit. Four built-in themes are supported: light, dark, light-hc (high contrast), and dark-hc. Themes are applied as data attributes on the root element and all CSS tokens (colors, shadows, borders) are resolved via CSS custom properties (--g-color-*, --g-text-*, etc.), enabling runtime switching without page reload.
system.spacing: Spacing uses a 4px base unit scale exposed as CSS custom properties (--g-spacing-*). Components accept semantic size props ('xs', 's', 'm', 'l', 'xl') that map to the scale. Layout primitives (Box, Flex, Container, Row, Col) accept spacing shorthand props that reference the same tokens.
system.typography: Typography is managed via the Text component and CSS custom properties (--g-text-*). Pre-defined text variants cover display, header (subheader-1 through subheader-3), body, caption, and code styles. Font family, weight, size, and line-height are all token-driven. The system supports both LTR and RTL text directions via the ThemeProvider direction prop.

libraries.purpose: AI chat and assistant interface component library providing chat containers, message bubbles, prompt inputs, tool call displays, rating blocks, and smart scroll hooks.
libraries.not_for: Not for general messaging or social chat — purpose-built for AI assistant/LLM interfaces. Not for standard form inputs — use uikit.

libraries.purpose: Server-side HTML render function factory with plugin architecture for injecting scripts, styles, theme attributes, analytics, and microfrontend version info into SSR page shells.
libraries.not_for: Not for client-side rendering — server-side (Node.js) only. Not for streaming-agnostic setups without the plugin API.

libraries.purpose: Base Axios HTTP client class with auto-cancellation of concurrent requests, CSRF token management, and optional request/error collection for debugging.
libraries.not_for: Not for direct use — subclass it to define domain-specific API methods. Not for browser-unaware server environments.

libraries.purpose: Shared Babel preset bundling preset-env, preset-react, preset-typescript, decorator support, and runtime helpers for Gravity UI projects.
libraries.not_for: Not for projects with radically different browser targets or non-standard decorator proposals without config overrides.

libraries.purpose: Ready-made blog CMS toolkit providing feed headers, post cards, post metadata, pagination, search, and layout wrappers for building blog listing and detail pages.
libraries.not_for: Not a general content framework — purpose-built for blog/article list and post pages. Not for application UI or landing pages — use uikit or page-constructor respectively.

libraries.purpose: Shared Browserslist configuration targeting modern browsers (last 2 major versions, ES6, Firefox ESR) for consistent autoprefixer and babel-preset-env targets.
libraries.not_for: Not for projects needing IE11 or legacy browser support. Not suitable for highly customized browser audiences without extension.

libraries.purpose: Unified chart wrapper component with plugin registry, error boundary, and loading fallback. Default entry point for charts — wraps @gravity-ui/charts (D3/SVG) and @gravity-ui/yagr (canvas timeseries) via the plugin system.
libraries.not_for: Not for using a specific chart library directly without the plugin abstraction — use @gravity-ui/charts or @gravity-ui/yagr directly in that case.

libraries.purpose: D3/SVG declarative chart library for line, bar, area, scatter, pie, and other chart types with automatic resize handling and full TypeScript types.
libraries.not_for: Not for canvas-based high-performance timeseries — use @gravity-ui/yagr. Not when chart type is managed via a plugin registry — use chartkit.

libraries.purpose: Extended component library supplementing uikit with higher-level components: adaptive tabs, changelog dialog, confirm dialog, cookie consent, notifications, onboarding menu, reactions, share popover, stories, and more.
libraries.not_for: Not a replacement for uikit — extends it with app-level patterns. Not for AI interfaces (use aikit) or navigation shell (use navigation).

libraries.purpose: Widget dashboard builder with drag-and-drop resizable grid, plugin widget system, and ActionPanel for adding widgets. For configurable user dashboards with DnD layout.
libraries.not_for: Not for static dashboard layouts — use CSS grid/flex. Not for simple page layouts — the plugin registration overhead is only justified for user-configurable widget grids.

libraries.purpose: React-query wrapper providing typed data source definitions, tag-based cache invalidation, DataLoader/DataInfiniteLoader state components, and the ClientDataManager.
libraries.not_for: Not a replacement for direct react-query — complements it with conventions for Gravity UI apps. Not for mutation-only patterns without query caching needs.

libraries.purpose: Date picker and calendar components: Calendar, DatePicker, DateField, RangeCalendar, RangeDatePicker, RelativeDatePicker, and related range/relative variants. uikit does not include date selection components.
libraries.not_for: Not for date arithmetic or formatting without UI — use @gravity-ui/date-utils. Not for timezone utilities — use @gravity-ui/date-utils.

libraries.purpose: Date utility library built on dayjs providing DateTime creation, parsing (including relative expressions like 'now-1d'), timezone utilities, duration, and locale settings.
libraries.not_for: Not for rendering date pickers — use @gravity-ui/date-components. Not a React component library — pure utility functions.

libraries.purpose: Form dialog component (DFDialog) backed by react-final-form with typed field controls (text, checkbox, select, radio, tumbler, multi-text, editable lists) and tabbed layout support.
libraries.not_for: Not for individual form controls outside dialog context — use uikit form components. Not for simple single-field confirmations — use a plain Dialog with a single input.

libraries.purpose: JSON-spec-driven form configuration engine that renders complete forms from a schema, supporting arrays, objects, accordions, and 30+ input types.
libraries.not_for: Not for individual form controls — use uikit for standard forms. Not for dialog-based forms — use @gravity-ui/dialog-fields. Only justified when the form structure itself must be data-driven.

libraries.purpose: Shared ESLint flat config enforcing the Gravity UI styleguide with TypeScript rules and optional sub-configs for Prettier, a11y, and import order.
libraries.not_for: Not for projects with conflicting custom ESLint rules. Prettier peer dependency required for the prettier sub-config.

libraries.purpose: Server-side only. Lightweight Express.js wrapper integrating with NodeKit for structured request logging, distributed tracing, async middleware, CSRF, CSP headers, and declarative route definitions.
libraries.not_for: Not for browser/frontend code — server-side Node.js only. Not for non-NodeKit Express apps — requires a NodeKit instance.

libraries.purpose: Canvas + React graph/flow diagram library with Preact signals for reactive block state, ELK auto-layout, custom HTML block rendering, layers, and portals.
libraries.not_for: Not for charts or data visualizations — use chartkit/charts/yagr. Not for timeline visualizations — use @gravity-ui/timeline.

libraries.purpose: Lightweight keyset-based internationalization class with pluralization, template variable substitution, and TypeScript type-safe key access.
libraries.not_for: Not for ICU MessageFormat or React rich-text formatting — use @gravity-ui/i18n-react. Not for SSR with complex locale configs — use @gravity-ui/i18n-node.

libraries.purpose: SVG icon asset library providing hundreds of icon components for use with the uikit Icon component.
libraries.not_for: Not for illustrations or marketing images — use @gravity-ui/illustrations. Icons are SVG data, not self-contained visual components.

libraries.purpose: SVG illustration asset library providing themed empty-state and error-state illustrations (404, 403, 500, no-results, resource types) that respond to Gravity UI CSS theme tokens.
libraries.not_for: Not for functional UI icons — use @gravity-ui/icons. Not for marketing or landing-page imagery.

libraries.purpose: Full-featured markdown editor with WYSIWYG (ProseMirror) and markup (CodeMirror) modes, split preview, toolbar, and YFM (Yandex Flavored Markdown) extension support.
libraries.not_for: Not for read-only markdown rendering — use @diplodoc/transform or similar. Requires a useMarkdownEditor instance — not a drop-in textarea replacement.

libraries.purpose: App-level navigation shell providing AsideHeader (collapsible sidebar), MobileHeader (burger menu), Footer, Settings panel, HotkeysPanel, PageLayout, and ActionBar for application-level navigation structures.
libraries.not_for: Not for in-page navigation (tabs, breadcrumbs) — use uikit Tabs and Breadcrumbs. Not for landing pages — use page-constructor.

libraries.purpose: Server-side only. Node.js application toolkit providing config loading, structured pino logging, OpenTelemetry tracing, graceful shutdown, and hierarchical request context (AppContext).
libraries.not_for: Not for browser/frontend code — server-side Node.js only. Not for simple scripts that do not need logging or tracing infrastructure.

libraries.purpose: For landing/marketing pages only. Block-based page builder providing 40+ content blocks (headers, media, cards, forms, tables, maps) for composing marketing and documentation pages from YAML/JSON configs.
libraries.not_for: Not for application UI — use uikit. Not for blog pages — use blog-constructor. Not for dynamic server-rendered app pages.

libraries.purpose: CLI tool for building static HTML pages from YAML configurations using @gravity-ui/page-constructor with webpack, watch mode, custom components, navigation, and analytics.
libraries.not_for: Not suitable for dynamic server-rendered applications — output is purely static HTML. Not for development-mode translation workflows.

libraries.purpose: Shared Prettier formatting configuration enforcing the Gravity UI code style for consistent formatting across all packages.
libraries.not_for: Not for projects with different Prettier rules. Prettier must be installed separately as a peer dependency.

libraries.purpose: Shared Stylelint configuration for CSS/SCSS linting with SCSS support, optional Prettier integration, and CSS property ordering.
libraries.not_for: Not for projects on older Node.js (<20) or Stylelint (<16.18.0) versions.

libraries.purpose: Advanced TanStack Table v8 wrapper for virtualization, tree rows, row reordering via drag-and-drop, column pinning, and sticky headers. For simple tables use uikit Table.
libraries.not_for: Not for simple read-only tables — use uikit Table. Not without calling useTable first — the component requires a TanStack Table instance.

libraries.purpose: Canvas-based timeline/Gantt visualization with events, markers, sections, axes, zoom, and pan via TimelineCanvas React component and useTimeline hook.
libraries.not_for: Not for graph/flow diagrams — use @gravity-ui/graph. Not for bar/line/area charts — use chartkit or charts.

libraries.purpose: Shared TypeScript base configuration with strict mode, noImplicitReturns, noUnusedLocals, ES2017 target, and CommonJS modules.
libraries.not_for: Not for browser-only ESM builds or projects targeting a different ES version without overriding compilerOptions.

libraries.purpose: Core component library for building application UI. Contains buttons, inputs, dialogs, tables, layout primitives, overlays, data display, and typography components.
libraries.not_for: Not for landing/marketing pages — use page-constructor. Not for AI chat interfaces — use aikit. Not for date pickers — use date-components. Not for advanced virtualized tables — use @gravity-ui/table.

libraries.purpose: Webpack 5 plugin that inlines i18n translation strings into production bundles and generates per-locale asset files in a single build pass.
libraries.not_for: Not for development-mode translation. Not for Webpack 4 projects. Only supports string literal i18n keys, not template strings.

libraries.purpose: Canvas-based high-performance timeseries chart renderer built on uPlot. Use for real-time or large-dataset timeseries visualizations without ChartKit plugin overhead.
libraries.not_for: Not for SVG/D3-level customization — use @gravity-ui/charts. Not for simple one-off charts without timeseries — use charts. Not when chart type is managed via a plugin registry — use chartkit.

categories.actions: Components for triggering actions — buttons, links, dropdowns, clipboard, and action toolbars
categories.ai: AI assistant and chat interface components — message bubbles, prompt inputs, tool call displays, rating
categories.data-display: Components for presenting structured data — tables, lists, definition lists, charts, progress, pagination
categories.feedback: Status and notification components — alerts, toasts, loaders, spinners, skeletons, placeholders
categories.forms: Form input and selection components — text inputs, selects, checkboxes, radios, sliders, file inputs
categories.layout: Structural layout and spacing primitives — flex, grid, containers, dividers, cards, boxes
categories.navigation: In-page and app-level navigation — breadcrumbs, tabs, sidebar, mobile header, footer, toc
categories.overlays: Floating UI components — modals, dialogs, popovers, popups, tooltips, sheets, drawers
categories.typography: Text rendering components — headings, body text, labels, links, hotkeys
categories.utility: Infrastructure and behavior components — portals, copy-to-clipboard, file drop zones, disclosure
